source_pr.title: gc_worker: use delete range to replace unsafe destroy range when multi-rocksdb is enabled
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #42410

Problem Summary:
To handle  with the problem described in the issue, this PR change the unsafe destroy range behaviour by using normal destroy range when raftstore-v2 is enabled.

### What is changed and how it works?

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code



### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: Unit test assertions relied on an implicit/stable ordering of returned stores / sent requests, but the underlying iteration/order was not guaranteed, leading to intermittent mismatches.
failure_signature: Order-dependent test failure: comparing slices of stores/requests without sorting (non-deterministic order)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it compared slices whose order was not guaranteed (e.g., stores returned from cluster state and the collected sent requests). Depending on iteration/scheduling, the same elements could appear in different orders, causing intermittent assertion failures. This PR stabilizes the test by sorting `stores` by address and sorting collected sent requests by key/address before checking, making the assertions order-independent.
fix_pattern: Before asserting equality on slices produced from unordered sources, sort them deterministically (e.g., by address/key) or compare as sets to avoid relying on incidental iteration order.

evidence: evidence: sort.Slice(stores, func(i, j int) bool { return stores[i].Address < stores[j].Address }) (store/gcworker/gc_worker_test.go)

files: ddl/delete_range.go, ddl/util/BUILD.bazel, ddl/util/util.go, parser/BUILD.bazel, parser/parser.go, parser/parser.go.committed, store/gcworker/BUILD.bazel, store/gcworker/gc_worker.go, store/gcworker/gc_worker_test.go
